description: A cognitive neuroscience laboratory has collected Tower of London (ToL) task data across six difficulty levels (2-7 moves) to assess planning efficiency. Using the dataset at ~/pebl/data/tower_of_london_data.csv, generate a planning efficiency report saved to ~/pebl/analysis/tol_report.json. The analysis requires excluding participants with an overall mean first-move latency under 50ms. For valid participants, calculate the proportion_optimal (mean of solved_optimal), mean_excess_moves (mean of actual_moves - optimal_moves), and mean_planning_time_ms (mean of first_move_latency_ms) for each difficulty level (2-7), along with a planning_slope_ms_per_move derived from a linear regression of mean planning time on optimal moves. The output JSON must contain a 'participants' list (including 'id', 'by_difficulty' metrics keyed "2" through "7", 'planning_slope_ms_per_move', and exclusion status/reason), as well as 'group_means' and 'group_mean_planning_slope_ms_per_move' averaged across all valid participants.
